description:
## Goal

End-to-end acceptance that proves the shared zone works under realistic spawn-and-share traffic across all three execution paths.

## Depends on

fz-cty.1 (lift-cap), fz-cty.5 (bitstring policy).

## Scenario

A sender process creates one large bitstring (≥ 128 bytes — well above threshold). It spawns N receivers and sends the bitstring to each. Each receiver receives the message, optionally does some work that triggers GC, then exits.

Expected behaviour:

- Exactly **one** SharedBin is ever allocated (live count peaks at 1 across the entire run).
- SharedBin refcount climbs to 1 + N as sends complete, then drops to zero as receivers exit.
- Across mid-flight GCs in each receiver, the receiver's MSO entry is correctly updated to the to-space copy of its ProcBin (no UAF, no leak).
- Across receiver process drop, the SharedBin refcount decrements correctly.

## Implementation

Add `tests/shared_heap_acceptance.rs` (or equivalent in the existing spec_parity / test_runner infrastructure). Test runs the scenario under:
- Interpreter (`ir_interp`)
- JIT (current default Cranelift path)
- AOT (`aot_shim`)

Each path runs the same fz source. Assertions:
- `shared_bin_live_count()` (from .2) is exactly 1 at peak, 0 at end.
- All N receivers observe the expected bytes (round-trip integrity).
- Optional: instrument MSO list sizes to assert expected counts per process.

## Tests

The acceptance test itself. May discover that one of the runtime paths needs a small adjustment to expose `shared_bin_live_count` for assertion; if so, surface as a tightly-scoped change inside this ticket (no new behaviour).

## Verification

- Test passes in all three paths.
- Refcount invariant holds across GC cycles and process death.
- No deadlocks, no panics, no leaks.
title: fz-shr.6: Three-path acceptance — shared-bin survives spawn-and-share
